title: baccaurea microcarpa
intro: Baccaurea microcarpa, also known as the Tawau oak, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It is native to Borneo and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and various skin conditions. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it beneficial for cardiovascular health and as an anti-aging agent. Baccaurea microcarpa is often used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues and promote overall well-being.
